title: poa calotheca
intro: Poa calotheca, also known as Poa annua, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various digestive issues, such as diarrhea, stomach pain, and inflammation. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the stomach and intestines, reducing the risk of infection and promoting overall health. In herbal preparations, Poa calotheca is often used in the form of tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
